title: Go JSON Encoding
content:
### JSON Encoding

The `encoding/json` package provides JSON encoding and decoding.

### Marshaling (Go to JSON)

```go
import "encoding/json"

type Person struct {
    Name string `json:"name"`
    Age  int    `json:"age"`
}

p := Person{Name: "Alice", Age: 30}
data, err := json.Marshal(p)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
fmt.Println(string(data)) // {"name":"Alice","age":30}
```

### Pretty Print JSON

```go
data, err := json.MarshalIndent(p, "", "  ")
fmt.Println(string(data))
```

### Unmarshaling (JSON to Go)

```go
jsonStr := `{"name":"Bob","age":25}`
var p Person
err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(jsonStr), &p)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
fmt.Println(p.Name) // Bob
fmt.Println(p.Age)  // 25
```

### JSON with Dynamic Keys

```go
var data map[string]any
err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(`{"key": "value", "num": 42}`), &data)
fmt.Println(data["key"]) // "value"
```

### JSON with Slices

```go
jsonStr := `[{"name":"Alice"},{"name":"Bob"}]`
var people []Person
err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(jsonStr), &people)
```

### JSON Streams (Encoder/Decoder)

```go
file, _ := os.Create("output.json")
defer file.Close()

encoder := json.NewEncoder(file)
encoder.SetIndent("", "  ")
encoder.Encode(p)

// Reading
file2, _ := os.Open("input.json")
defer file2.Close()

decoder := json.NewDecoder(file2)
var p2 Person
decoder.Decode(&p2)
```

### Custom JSON Marshaling

Implement `json.Marshaler` and `json.Unmarshaler`:

```go
type Color struct {
    R, G, B uint8
}

func (c Color) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
    return json.Marshal(fmt.Sprintf("#%02X%02X%02X", c.R, c.G, c.B))
}

func (c *Color) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
    var s string
    if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &s); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    // parse hex string...
    return nil
}
```

> **Key Point:** Only exported fields (capitalized) are JSON-encoded. Use struct tags to control field names and behavior. Use `json.NewEncoder`/`json.NewDecoder` for streaming large JSON data.

quiz.question: What tag option omits a field if it has a zero value?
